conclusion: all three candidates failed prereg success rules; intraday independent baseline not viable under current contract

candidates.notes: best of the three across return, drawdown, turnover; but still fails overall

failure_analysis.root_cause: Systemic low-invested-weight under D1-D5+TopK+cash-retention contract. All candidates held 81-84% cash on average during 2019-2021 bull market (benchmark +82%), making positive absolute relative returns structurally impossible regardless of signal quality.
failure_analysis.signal_quality_note: All three candidates consistently improved upon v18 reference across return, drawdown, and turnover deltas, suggesting intraday microstructure signals contain genuine information. The failure is in absolute return/perturbation gates, not in relative reference comparison.
failure_analysis.contract_interaction: The low invested_weight (~15-18%) is inherent to the current TopK=10+multi-cohort contract for single-family baselines. v18 itself has avg_invested_weight=0.155. A different portfolio contract (e.g., higher TopK, different cohort count, or weight-mapping rules) could produce materially different results.

interpretation_note: 本轮失败不应被解读为 intraday microstructure signals 无信息，而应被解读为：在当前 D1-D5 + TopK + 现金保留 + 不补位合同下，intraday independent baseline 未满足 prereg success rules。
next_directions_for_consideration: Higher invested-weight contract design: test intraday signals under a contract that allocates more capital (e.g., single cohort, full-investment, or weight-mapping with concentration)
Portfolio extraction interaction: does a different head-extraction or refresh rule combined with intraday signals produce better perturbation/cost robustness?
Intraday signals as additive layer: re-test intraday signals as additions to a higher-invested-weight baseline (not as standalone family)
